nodes.label: P4.3-decay — why does OURS decay with depth? (width is not the lever)
nodes.rationale: Widening (fix64 vs widen to W240) does NOT fix the depth decay — dead-frac≈0, higher effective rank buys no accuracy — so capacity is refuted; the decay is the per-layer local contrast objective drifting the representation off the class manifold past ~layer 5, confirmed by the mixed-task corruption of an early-solved flat subtask.

nodes.label: Fixed-width W64 control refutes capacity/width as the depth-decay cause
nodes.rationale: fix64 (W64 constant) and widen (up to W240 at L12) decay to the SAME accuracy; dead-unit fraction ~0.00 at every layer kills the dead-neuron hypothesis; widen carries higher effective rank (18.7 vs 12.3) at identical accuracy, so lost rank is a symptom, not the binding constraint — width is decisively not the lever.

nodes.label: Named mechanism: per-layer local contrast drifts the representation off the class manifold
nodes.rationale: Every layer applies its own local InfoNCE with no signal to preserve already-class-relevant structure, so repeated local transforms steer the representation toward an informative-but-not-class-aligned code — separability peaks (~L5) then erodes, independent of width; a direction failure, not a capacity failure.

nodes.label: All-tap/boosting readout vindicated as the deployed fix (pending P5)
nodes.rationale: Since decay is intrinsic to stacking the local objective, the P4-era fix is preservation not capacity: reading the ~L5 peak via all-tap/boosting instead of the decayed top; residual/skip connections flagged as the clean architectural candidate; useful composition depth pinned at ~5 layers for this cell.

nodes.label: Mixed flat+headroom task: the corruption detector
nodes.rationale: An iso-budget task combining an easy flat subtask (solved by layer 2-3) with a headroom subtask reveals that OURS's deep layers actively CORRUPT the early-solved flat subtask (decays to ~0.51 by L12) while tuned BP holds it flat (~0.75) — the cleanest, OURS-specific decay tell, later promoted into Phase 5's p5lib as make_mixed.
